page: Vultr vhf-8c-32gb is a High Frequency Compute server configured with 8 vCPUs and 8 physical cores powered by an Intel Core Processor based on the Skylake architecture running at 2.0 GHz. The CPU allocation is shared, and the system features 32.0 GB of memory, translating to 4.0 GB of RAM per core. Local storage is bundled with a 512 GB NVMe SSD, and the network configuration includes one complimentary public IPv4 address. No dedicated GPU is present on this instance type.

Benchmark results indicate strong single-core CPU performance, with stress-ng single-core scoring in the top 25% and single-threaded gzip compression reaching top-tier status in the top 10%. However, multi-core performance is average, and memory bandwidth for large uncached blocks falls into the bottom 25% for both read and write operations. Passmark memory testing shows top-tier performance, while database operations, Redis SET operations, and static web serving throughput remain in the average tier. LLM inference speeds are average for small models but drop to the bottom 10% for medium models.

The shared CPU allocation and average multi-core performance represent a tradeoff for workloads requiring sustained parallel processing, but the server offers high single-threaded efficiency and a balanced memory-to-vCPU ratio. This configuration is suitable for single-threaded processing tasks, lightweight database operations, static web serving, and general-purpose applications that do not require dedicated CPU resources or intensive large-block memory bandwidth.
description: Vultr vhf-8c-32gb is a High Frequency Compute server featuring 8 shared Intel vCPUs on the Skylake architecture clocked at 2.0 GHz, 32.0 GB of RAM, and a 512 GB NVMe SSD. It delivers strong single-core CPU performance, with single-threaded gzip compression scoring in the top 10% and stress-ng single-core in the top 25%. However, multi-core performance is average, and large-block uncached memory bandwidth is weak, placing in the bottom 25%. The server lacks a GPU. This hardware profile is suited for workloads such as static web serving, lightweight database operations, and single-threaded data processing where shared CPU resources are acceptable.
og_description: Vultr vhf-8c-32gb is a High Frequency Compute server with 8 shared Intel vCPUs, 32 GB RAM, and a 512 GB NVMe SSD, offering strong single-core performance alongside average multi-core capabilities.
meta_description: Vultr vhf-8c-32gb is a High Frequency Compute server featuring 8 shared Intel vCPUs, 32 GB RAM, and a 512 GB NVMe SSD for general-purpose workloads.
tagline: An x86_64 server with shared Intel processors, 32 GB RAM, and NVMe storage, delivering strong single-threaded performance for general workloads.
bullet_points: 8 shared Intel vCPUs based on the Skylake architecture running at 2.0 GHz
32.0 GB of system memory and 512 GB NVMe SSD storage
Strong single-core CPU performance and top-tier single-threaded compression
Weak large-block uncached memory read and write bandwidth
Suited for static web serving, lightweight databases, and general-purpose tasks
